_note: Demo citizen account seeds — INTENTIONALLY EMPTY. Phase 1.5 originally seeded a fixed list of 60 citizen accounts (50 FL clusters + 10 out-of-state) with a shared password so reviewers could exercise the engagement loop (likes, dislikes, comments, geography-scoped poll views). That approach was retired when the self-serve demo-citizen signup flow shipped (POST /api/citizen-auth/demo-signup): any visitor now mints their own demo account with a display name + state + district of their choice, no shared inbox, no 60-account cap, real engagement attribution. Real verified citizen accounts will replace this entirely once the ID.me Relying Party contract is funded.
_history: If you need to restore the original 60-account seed list for local development, the git history at HEAD~ contains the previous payload. The seeded emails followed the pattern <name>@civicview-voters.com — the fresh-start wipe (CIVICVIEW_WIPE_REP_DEMO) targets that domain specifically so it can clean up Render Postgres rows from before the retirement without touching self-serve demo accounts (which use @demo-citizens.civicview.app).
